title: Koellensteinia alba
taxonomy: Koellensteinia alba is a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae. The family Orchidaceae is the largest and one of the most diverse families of flowering plants. Koellensteinia alba is classified within the subfamily Orchidoideae and tribe Cranichideae. The subfamily Orchidoideae includes about 20,000 species of orchids, which are further divided into several tribes. The classification of Koellensteinia alba reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history within the orchid family.

intro: Koellensteinia alba, also known as White Koellensteinia, is a species of flowering plant in the family Commelinaceae, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, respiratory problems, and digestive disorders, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Koellensteinia alba include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Koellensteinia alba is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is used to treat coughs, colds, and other respiratory conditions, as well as to aid in digestion and reduce inflammation.
